description: Deferred review items tracked across all phases. Each entry maps to a source review comment that was deemed reasonable but deferred to a later phase. Check this file at each phase integration gate to identify items that may have been resolved as a side effect of other work.

items.item: Unify db.ts + store-driver.ts SQLite connections into single factory
items.detail: createStoreDriver() and createDb() each open their own bun:sqlite connection. If transaction() callback tries to write through a Drizzle client from createDb(), it will hit SQLITE_BUSY because BEGIN IMMEDIATE holds a write lock. Fix: consolidate into a single connection factory or add connection pooling.

items.item: Add proper transaction isolation test using shared connection handle
items.detail: Test bypasses driver.transaction() - performs raw SQL insert outside the transaction callback. After connection unification (DEF-001), add a test that inserts via the driver-managed connection within a transaction callback and verifies commit/rollback semantics.

items.item: Populate killedByGrace field in ExitInfo when process is killed via grace-period SIGTERM→SIGKILL chain
items.detail: ExitInfo interface defines killedByGrace?: boolean but the field is never set. The doKill() flow sends SIGTERM first then SIGKILL after graceMs, but Exited promise always returns killedByGrace: undefined. Fix: track whether SIGKILL was sent after grace period expiry and expose it in ExitInfo. This becomes important in Phase 3 when downstream error classification (L1-L4) needs to distinguish between direct kills and grace-period timeouts for proper diagnostic messages.

items.item: Preserve doKill trigger source in ExitInfo for diagnostic and logging purposes
items.detail: doKill(trigger: string, forceSignal?: SignalName) receives trigger values ('timeout', 'abort_signal', 'manual_kill') but never stores or propagates them to ExitInfo. When Phase 3 implements real backend subprocess orchestration, the trigger source is needed for proper L1-L4 error classification and user-facing abort reason messages (e.g. 'killed by timeout' vs 'killed by user abort'). Fix: add trigger field to ExitInfo and populate it from doKill calls.
